# Compound Output Templates

This document defines the output templates for knowledge documents
generated by compound.

## Common Frontmatter Specification

All compound-generated files must include YAML frontmatter:

```yaml
---
last_compound_scan: YYYY-MM-DD
compound_scan_mode: full|incremental
---
```

- `last_compound_scan`: Date of the last compound scan
- `compound_scan_mode`: Scan mode (full or incremental)

**Do NOT add a second "> Auto-generated by ttadk, last updated: {DATE}" line under the title.** The frontmatter already carries the date; repeating it as a blockquote is duplication.

## Common Table of Contents Section

> **Heading stability rule:** Top-level `##` section headings are controlled by the coordinator — never copied verbatim from agent output. This ensures cross-reference anchors remain stable across runs. Only `###` and deeper headings are generated freely by agents.

Insert a table of contents section after frontmatter and before the main content headings. TOC
entries are generated from the actual sections AI produced (null-value passthrough applied — only sections with content appear).

```markdown
## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Creational Patterns](#creational-patterns)
- [Structural Patterns](#structural-patterns)
- [Behavioral Patterns](#behavioral-patterns)
- [Pattern Dependencies](#pattern-dependencies)
```

> Note: TOC entries must exactly match the actual section headings in
> the document. When the null-value passthrough rule is in effect,
> omitted sections do not appear in the TOC.

## Source Tracing Format

Each knowledge entry ends with a single `**Source**: ...` line. Scan time lives in the file's
frontmatter (`last_compound_scan`), not per entry. Constitution-style dimensions (`output.style: constitution`) do not use source tracing markers.

```markdown
### Strategy Pattern

The strategy pattern is used in AIToolConfig for...

**Source**: `services/ttadk_cli/src/ai-tools/ai-tool-config.ts` [Code Direct]
```

### Source Marker Types

| Marker | Meaning | Confidence | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `[Code Direct]` | Extracted directly from source code | High | When scanning specific code that can be directly referenced |
| `[AI Inferred]` | AI deduced from code structure, no direct line correspondence | Medium | When inferring design intent from code structure |
| `[External Ref]` | Links to external documentation only | Low | When referencing external API docs or third-party library docs |

### Legacy Marker Aliases

The following Chinese-language markers from earlier compound versions are treated as aliases:

| Legacy Marker | Equivalent New Marker |
|---|---|
| `[代码直引]` | `[Code Direct]` |
| `[AI 推断]` | `[AI Inferred]` |

Stale detection, merge key computation, and upgrade attempts apply identically to both forms.

### Source Tracing Examples

```markdown
### Factory Pattern

LoaderFactory coordinates the creation of 6 loader types...

**Source**: `services/ttadk_cli/src/loader/loader-factory.ts` [Code Direct]

---

### Module Decoupling Inference

Based on code structure, LoaderFactory's design intent is...

**Source**: AI Deduction [AI Inferred]
```

### Verification Markers

When Phase 3.1 source verification passes, append `[Verified]`:
```markdown
**Source**: `services/ttadk_cli/src/loader/loader-factory.ts` [Code Direct] [Verified]
```

When verification fails, append `[Pending]`:
```markdown
**Source**: `services/ttadk_cli/src/loader/loader-factory.ts` [Code Direct] [Pending]
```

### Merge Key Rules

When Agent C merges contributions from multiple agents:

- `[Code Direct]` merge key: `(normalized_source_file, heading_text)`
- All source file paths must be normalized to repo-root-relative format before merge key computation
- `[AI Inferred]` merge key: `(dimension_name, heading_text)`

---

## Null-Value Passthrough Rule

If a section's scan results cannot provide incremental value beyond
"reading the source code directly" (e.g., merely restating code
comments, vague descriptions, inferences without concrete evidence),
omit the section entirely — write nothing.

This is the core constraint against AI completion bias (the tendency
to fill content).

**Judgment Criteria**:
- If a reader could get equivalent information by reading source code
  directly → omit
- If a description has conclusions but no evidence → omit
- If content says "the project uses X" without showing how → omit

**File-level passthrough**: If an entire dimension produces no signal
(all sections would be empty or "待填充" placeholders), do NOT emit a
skeleton file. An empty file pollutes the index and wastes SDD-loading
context. The only exception is dimensions with `output.style:
constitution` — those must always emit (constitution is authoritative
even when sparse). Also, do NOT emit files for dimensions not declared
in schema.yaml.

---

## Code Template Prohibition

Compound outputs MUST NOT contain runnable code templates or how-to
snippets that a reader could obtain by copying any existing file that
already follows the pattern.

Rejected output shapes:
- "How to add a new X" step-by-step guides with fill-in code blocks
- Full skeleton implementations of Service/Handler/Factory/Strategy
- Inline framework interface definitions (e.g., restating the Service
  interface a reader already reads from the framework source)

Accepted replacements (signal, not catalog):
- The invariants the template would embody: "every domain service
  must have `checkRequest()`; error path must flow through
  `GenStatusCode → WithStatusCode`"
- The gotchas a copy-paste reader will miss: "TCC init failure
  panics; the `type:\"json\"` tag is not optional for complex types"
- The cross-service divergence: "`GrayConfig.IsInGray` uses `<=` in
  task service but `<` in matchmaking — align with the service you
  are editing"

If a section would consist mostly of code blocks, it almost certainly
violates this rule — reframe it as invariants + pitfalls and drop the
code.

---

## Template List

### 1. docs/arch/patterns.md (Design Patterns)

> Structure aligns with schema.yaml `Design Patterns` dimension

```markdown
---
last_compound_scan: {DATE}
compound_scan_mode: {MODE}
---

# Design Patterns


## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Creational Patterns](#creational-patterns)  <!-- required: false, only if content exists -->
- [Structural Patterns](#structural-patterns)  <!-- required: false, only if content exists -->
- [Behavioral Patterns](#behavioral-patterns)  <!-- required: false, only if content exists -->
- [Pattern Dependencies](#pattern-dependencies)  <!-- required: false, only if content exists -->

## Overview

{Overall overview of project design patterns}

## Creational Patterns

### {Pattern Name}

{Pattern description and how it is used}

**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct]

## Structural Patterns

{Same format as above, required=false, only if content exists}

## Behavioral Patterns

{Same format as above, required=false, only if content exists}

## Pattern Dependencies

{Dependencies and relationships between patterns, required=false, only if content exists}
```

### 2. docs/CODING.md (Coding Standards)

> `output.style: constitution` — use the constitution template (Section 10-14 and `templates/constitution/CODING.md`), not a generic `##` structure. Do NOT include source tracing markers.

### 3. docs/references/fixes.md (Common Issues & Defensive Patterns)

> This template overrides the generic reference template for fixes.md,
> adding categorized sections for Known Defects and Defensive Coding Patterns.

```markdown
---
last_compound_scan: {DATE}
compound_scan_mode: {MODE}
---

# Common Issues & Defensive Patterns


## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Known Defects](#known-defects)  <!-- required: false -->
  - [Active Issues](#active-issues)  <!-- required: false -->
  - [Resolved Issues](#resolved-issues)  <!-- required: false -->
- [Defensive Coding Patterns](#defensive-coding-patterns)  <!-- required: false -->
  - [Concurrency Safety](#concurrency-safety)  <!-- required: false -->
  - [Error Recovery](#error-recovery)  <!-- required: false -->
  - [Initialization Guards](#initialization-guards)  <!-- required: false -->

## Overview

{Overall overview of known defects and defensive patterns}

## Known Defects

### Active Issues

{Unresolved bugs with trigger conditions, impact, and workarounds}
**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct|AI Inferred]

### Resolved Issues

{Previously fixed bugs with root cause and resolution}
**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct]

## Defensive Coding Patterns

### Concurrency Safety

{Locks, mutexes, idempotency guards — patterns that PREVENT concurrency bugs}
**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct]

### Error Recovery

{Panic recovery, circuit breakers, fallback chains — patterns that PREVENT cascade failures}
**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct]

### Initialization Guards

{Fail-fast patterns, nil checks, config validation — patterns that PREVENT invalid state}
**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct]
```

### 4-6. Reference Document Templates (config-templates/testing-patterns/deployment)

> All reference document templates follow the same specification as
> patterns.md:
> - Table of contents section (generated from actual sections produced)
> - Structure field alignment (required=true must produce, required=false
>   only if content exists)
> - Source tracing markers on each entry
> - Null-value passthrough rule

```markdown
---
last_compound_scan: {DATE}
compound_scan_mode: {MODE}
---

# {Document Title}


## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [{Section 1}](#{Section 1 anchor})  <!-- required: false -->
- [{Section N}](#{Section N anchor})  <!-- required: true/false -->

## Overview

{Overall overview}

## {Section}

**Source**: `{file path}` [Code Direct|AI Inferred|External Ref]

{Section content, required=false sections are omitted if no content}
```

### 7-9. Other Index Files (docs/arch/index.md, docs/product-specs/index.md, docs/references/index.md)

```markdown
---
last_compound_scan: {DATE}
compound_scan_mode: {MODE}
---

# {Index Title}


## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Requirements List](#requirements-list)  <!-- or Reference List -->

## Overview

{Index overview}

## Requirements List

| Document | Path | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| {Name} | {Path} | {Description} |
```

### 10-14. Constitution Documents (CONSTITUTION/QUALITY/RELIABILITY/SECURITY/CODING)

> When a schema.yaml dimension has `output.style: constitution`, use this
> template instead of the generic one.
> Format reference: the corresponding files in
> `templates/constitution/{TYPE}.md`; that directory is the
> sole template source for constitution-style documents.
> Key differences: no TOC section, no source tracing markers, no source
> references section, uses ### I~N numbering instead of ## peer headings.

**Companion Notes**:

1. **Single Template Source**: `templates/constitution/{TYPE}.md`
   defines the constitution document skeleton; `output-templates.md` only
   explains compound write rules
2. **Frontmatter**: Compound-generated results retain
   `last_compound_scan` + `compound_scan_mode`
3. **Core Format Differences**: No `## Table of Contents`, no source
   tracing markers, Core Section uses `### I.` / `### II.` / `### III.`
   Roman numeral numbering
4. **Remaining Structure**: Fixed Rules / Governance / version line
   paragraph structure and naming follow the corresponding
   constitution/{TYPE}.md files
5. **Null Values and Cross-References**: Empty sections follow the
   global null-value passthrough rule; cross-references still use
   `> 🔗 See [Topic Name](relative-path#anchor)` where the path is
   relative to the current file's directory and the anchor matches a
   real heading in the target file
6. **Fixed Rules Evidence Line (MANDATORY)**: Every bullet/item in the
   `## Fixed Rules` section MUST end with an `Evidence:` line matching
   one of these four kinds:
    - `Evidence: grep "<pattern>" @ services/` — pattern searchable in
      the services/ tree.
    - `Evidence: glob "<pattern>"` — file-existence check by glob.
    - `Evidence: file "<path>"` — a specific file must exist.
    - `Evidence: none (<reason>)` — cannot be verified by pattern;
      reason MUST be `governance-only` (process rule, e.g. CI-required,
      code-review-required) or `cross-referenced-from-owning-doc` (rule
      body lives in another constitution-style doc; render the rule
      line as `> 🔗 See [Rule Name](other-doc#fixed-rules)` and the
      Evidence line below it).
   Example:
   ```markdown
   - **TypeScript Strict Mode**: CLI and Web both enforce `strict: true`.
     Evidence: grep `"strict":\s*true` @ services/
   - **Commit Workflow**: When the user requests a commit in natural
     language, run `/adk:commit`.
     Evidence: none (governance-only)
   ```
   When a rule's schema-level evidence does not match in the current
   `services/` tree, append a status marker on the rule line:
   `**Rule Name** [Pending Evidence]` (first miss) or
   `**Rule Name** [Orphan]` (second-consecutive miss, requires human
   confirm). The marker is surfaced verbatim by Agent C.

## Version Line Specification (all constitution-style documents use this uniformly)

Format: `{Type} v{version} -- {description}, {date}`

Examples:
- Coding Standards v2.0 -- extracted from compound scan of 2441 Go files, 2026-04-20
- Security Practices v2.0 -- extracted from compound scan of 12 microservices, 2026-04-20

Prohibited:
- ❌ `**Version**: 2.0.0 | **Ratified**: 2026-04-20 | **Last Amended**: 2026-04-20`
  (this is the old constitution template format; compound does not use it)

## Title Language Specification

Process uniformly based on `preferred_language` configuration:
- `zh`: Document H1 uses `{ProjectName} {Chinese type name}`
  - Examples: `# TestDemo 编码标准`, `# TestDemo 安全实践`
- `en`: Document H1 uses `{ProjectName} {English Type}`
  - Examples: `# TestDemo Coding Standards`

H2/H3 heading rules:
- Fixed structure headings (Core Principles, Fixed Rules, Governance): use current `preferred_language`
- Pattern names and technical terms: keep in English (e.g., Strategy Pattern, Factory Pattern)
